target.title: Proposal: add minimal agent-to-agent handoff protocol (Phase 1)
target.body:
## Summary
This draft PR proposes a minimal formal handoff protocol for agent-to-agent delegation.

The goal is to make `sessions_send`-style agent handoff explicitly observable and less vulnerable to silent ambiguity.

## Proposed Phase 1 scope
- add `handoff_id`
- return structured ack
- distinguish `delivered / accepted / queued / rejected`
- preserve visibility when control-only results appear
- add a basic append-only ledger

## Why this is small on purpose
This proposal deliberately does **not** include a full workflow engine, retries, cancellation, dashboarding, or database-backed orchestration.

It is intended as the smallest first-party protocol that makes multi-agent delegation reliable enough to reason about and verify.

## Motivation
In real usage, transport success and handling success can diverge. This proposal asks OpenClaw to make that gap explicit and first-class rather than leaving it as runtime convention or local patching.

## Included in this PR
- a proposal document only, to make the shape discussable before asking maintainers to accept a larger code delta

If maintainers like this direction, the next PR should contain the actual minimal source implementation plus tests.
